question: Estimate the relative progression-free survival effect of niraparib versus olaparib in platinum-sensitive recurrent ovarian cancer by using placebo as the common comparator.
method_summary: This page uses a simple Bucher-style anchored indirect comparison. Study AB is niraparib versus placebo, and the second study is entered in the opposite orientation as olaparib versus placebo (that is, C versus B). Because the second effect is C vs B rather than B vs C, the indirect comparison is computed as log(HR_AC) = log(HR_AB) - log(HR_CB).

study_ab.label: Study AB
study_ab.comparison: Niraparib vs Placebo (ENGOT-OV16/NOVA, germline BRCA cohort)
study_ab.notes: Reported median PFS 21.0 vs 5.5 months; HR 0.27 (95% CI 0.17-0.41).

study_bc.label: Study CB
study_bc.comparison: Olaparib vs Placebo (SOLO2/ENGOT-Ov21; entered as C vs B)
study_bc.notes: Reported median PFS 19.1 vs 5.5 months; HR 0.30 (95% CI 0.22-0.41).

assumption_checks: Both studies are maintenance trials in platinum-sensitive recurrent ovarian cancer with placebo as comparator.
The comparison is most defensible when restricted to BRCA-mutated populations, because the NOVA estimate used here is from the germline BRCA cohort and SOLO2 enrolled BRCA1/2-mutated patients.
This is still a crude indirect comparison because trial eligibility, prior therapy patterns, and BRCA subgroup definitions are not perfectly identical.
The estimate below uses published PFS hazard ratios only and does not apply MAIC, STC, or patient-level adjustment.

indirect_result.note: Interpreted as niraparib vs olaparib for PFS under a simple Bucher-style anchored comparison. Because the interval crosses 1.0, this crude indirect estimate does not show a clear difference between the two maintenance strategies.
